_note: DOUBT-PROBE (is `event` a distinct primitive, or sugar for a score-gated action?): stat x climbs. TWO independent one-shot transitions watch the SAME threshold x>0.5 — one via an EVENT (rising-edge), one via an ACTION with a guard-stat gate. If both fire exactly once at the same tick, then on EXPRESSIVENESS grounds an action can replicate an event's one-shot semantics -> the count 'six' is a slice, not an irreducible basis. (The real reason events still earn their keep is LOD: events skip coarse entities, plain rules/actions differ — that's a runtime distinction, not an expressiveness one.)
